title: Unvalidated Redirect and HTTP Header Injection Mitigation

steps.message: Verify the detected Open Redirect at /login. Test if the application accepts URL-encoded carriage-return (%0d) and line-feed (%0a) characters within the redirection parameter to check for HTTP header injection vulnerabilities (KB 7.3.3.1).
Update WAF rules to block external URLs in the 'redirect' parameter for the /login endpoint and filter %0d and %0a characters.

Apply code fix: Use a whitelist for allowed redirection targets or force relative paths. Sanitize input to prevent CRLF injection by stripping %0d and %0a before processing headers. Review JavaScript APIs like window.location.href for client-side redirect security (KB 13.1).

Review security-relevant configuration settings in the Java environment. Ensure security-constraint and login-config elements are correctly defined to protect administrative resources (KB Table 19-3).
Notify the CISO of the remediation status and confirm that the open redirect vulnerability has been patched to prevent phishing and session hijacking.
